params.name: ImageId
params.description: The ID of the AMI to launch

params.name: InstanceType
params.description: The instance type such as t2.micro, m5.large, c5.xlarge

params.name: MinCount
params.description: The minimum number of instances to launch

params.name: MaxCount
params.description: The maximum number of instances to launch

params.name: KeyName
params.description: The name of the key pair to use for SSH access

params.name: SecurityGroupId
params.description: The IDs of the security groups

params.name: SubnetId
params.description: The ID of the subnet to launch the instance into

items.docs: Launches the specified number of instances using an AMI for which you have permissions. You can specify a number of options, or leave the default options. When you launch an instance, it enters the pending state, then the running state.

params.name: InstanceId
params.description: The instance IDs to describe

params.name: Filter
params.description: Filters to apply to the results

params.name: MaxResults
params.description: Maximum number of results to return

params.name: NextToken
params.description: Token for the next page of results

items.docs: Describes the specified instances or all instances. Returns information about the instances including instance ID, instance type, launch time, placement, monitoring state, and more.

params.name: InstanceId
params.description: The IDs of the instances to start

items.docs: Starts an Amazon EBS-backed instance that you have previously stopped. Instances that use Amazon EBS volumes as their root devices can be quickly stopped and started.

params.name: InstanceId
params.description: The IDs of the instances to stop

params.name: Force
params.description: Forces the instances to stop without graceful shutdown

items.docs: Stops an Amazon EBS-backed instance. You can use the stopped instance to modify the root volume, change instance type, or attach additional EBS volumes.

params.name: InstanceId
params.description: The IDs of the instances to terminate

items.docs: Shuts down the specified instances. This operation is idempotent; if you terminate an instance more than once, each call succeeds. Terminated instances remain visible for a short period after termination.

params.name: InstanceId
params.description: The IDs of the instances to reboot

items.docs: Requests a reboot of the specified instances. This operation is asynchronous; it only queues a request to reboot the specified instances.

params.name: InstanceId
params.description: The instance IDs to describe

params.name: IncludeAllInstances
params.description: Include all instances regardless of running state

items.docs: Describes the status of the specified instances or all of your instances. Instance status includes status checks, scheduled events, and instance state information.

info.name: Amazon EC2 Create an AMI from an Instance

params.name: InstanceId
params.description: The ID of the instance to create the image from

params.name: Name
params.description: A name for the new image

params.name: Description
params.description: A description for the new image

params.name: NoReboot
params.description: If true, Amazon EC2 does not shut down the instance before creating the image

items.docs: Creates an Amazon EBS-backed AMI from an Amazon EBS-backed instance that is either running or stopped. The AMI is created with the root volume and any additional EBS volumes attached to the instance.

params.name: ImageId
params.description: The image IDs to describe

params.name: Owner
params.description: Scopes the results to images with the specified owners

params.name: Filter
params.description: Filters to apply to the results

items.docs: Describes the specified images (AMIs, AKIs, and ARIs) available to you or all of the images available to you. Images available include public images, private images owned by you, and private images owned by others for which you have explicit launch permissions.

params.name: ImageId
params.description: The ID of the AMI to deregister

items.docs: Deregisters the specified AMI. After you deregister an AMI, it cannot be used to launch new instances. Deregistering does not delete the AMI.

info.name: Amazon EC2 Create a Security Group

params.name: GroupName
params.description: The name of the security group

params.name: GroupDescription
params.description: A description for the security group

params.name: VpcId
params.description: The ID of the VPC for the security group

items.docs: Creates a security group. A security group acts as a virtual firewall for your instances to control inbound and outbound traffic.

params.name: GroupId
params.description: The IDs of the security groups to describe

params.name: GroupName
params.description: The names of the security groups to describe

items.docs: Describes the specified security groups or all of your security groups. A security group is for use with instances either in the EC2-Classic platform or in a specific VPC.

info.name: Amazon EC2 Add Inbound Security Group Rules

params.name: GroupId
params.description: The ID of the security group

params.name: IpProtocol
params.description: The IP protocol name (tcp, udp, icmp) or number

params.name: FromPort
params.description: The start of port range for TCP and UDP protocols

params.name: ToPort
params.description: The end of port range for TCP and UDP protocols

params.name: CidrIp
params.description: The IPv4 CIDR range

items.docs: Adds the specified inbound (ingress) rules to a security group. An inbound rule permits instances to receive traffic from the specified IPv4 or IPv6 CIDR address range, or from the instances associated with the specified security group.

info.name: Amazon EC2 Delete a Security Group

params.name: GroupId
params.description: The ID of the security group to delete

params.name: GroupName
params.description: The name of the security group to delete

items.docs: Deletes a security group. If you attempt to delete a security group that is associated with an instance, or is referenced by another security group, the operation fails.

info.name: Amazon EC2 Create a Key Pair

params.name: KeyName
params.description: A unique name for the key pair

params.name: KeyType
params.description: The type of key pair (rsa or ed25519)

items.docs: Creates an ED25519 or 2048-bit RSA key pair with the specified name. Amazon EC2 stores the public key and displays the private key for you to save to a file.

params.name: KeyName
params.description: The key pair names to describe

params.name: KeyPairId
params.description: The IDs of the key pairs

items.docs: Describes the specified key pairs or all of your key pairs.

info.name: Amazon EC2 Delete a Key Pair

params.name: KeyName
params.description: The name of the key pair to delete

params.name: KeyPairId
params.description: The ID of the key pair to delete

items.docs: Deletes the specified key pair by removing the public key from Amazon EC2.

info.name: Amazon EC2 Allocate an Elastic IP Address

params.name: Domain
params.description: Set to vpc to allocate the address for use with instances in a VPC

items.docs: Allocates an Elastic IP address to your AWS account. After you allocate the Elastic IP address you can associate it with an instance or network interface.

info.name: Amazon EC2 Associate an Elastic IP Address

params.name: AllocationId
params.description: The allocation ID for the Elastic IP address

params.name: InstanceId
params.description: The ID of the instance to associate with

params.name: NetworkInterfaceId
params.description: The ID of the network interface

items.docs: Associates an Elastic IP address with an instance or a network interface. This is an idempotent operation.

info.name: Amazon EC2 Release an Elastic IP Address

params.name: AllocationId
params.description: The allocation ID of the Elastic IP address

items.docs: Releases the specified Elastic IP address. After releasing an Elastic IP address, it is released to the IP address pool.

info.name: Amazon EC2 Create a Launch Template

params.name: LaunchTemplateName
params.description: A name for the launch template

params.name: VersionDescription
params.description: A description for the first version of the launch template

items.docs: Creates a launch template. A launch template contains the parameters to launch an instance. When you launch an instance using RunInstances, you can specify a launch template instead of providing the launch parameters in the request.

params.name: LaunchTemplateId
params.description: The IDs of the launch templates

params.name: LaunchTemplateName
params.description: The names of the launch templates

items.docs: Describes one or more launch templates.

params.name: SpotPrice
params.description: The maximum price per unit hour for a Spot Instance

params.name: InstanceCount
params.description: The maximum number of Spot Instances to launch

params.name: Type
params.description: The Spot Instance request type

items.docs: Creates a Spot Instance request. For more information about Spot Instances, see Spot Instances in the Amazon EC2 User Guide.

info.name: Amazon EC2 Describe Spot Instance Requests

params.name: SpotInstanceRequestId
params.description: The IDs of the Spot Instance requests

items.docs: Describes the specified Spot Instance requests. You can use DescribeSpotInstanceRequests to find a running Spot Instance by querying for the Spot Instance request ID.

params.name: RegionName
params.description: The names of the Regions

params.name: AllRegions
params.description: Indicates whether to display all Regions including disabled

items.docs: Describes the Regions that are enabled for your account, or all Regions. For a list of the Regions supported by Amazon EC2, see the Amazon EC2 Regions and Endpoints.

params.name: ZoneName
params.description: The names of the Availability Zones

params.name: ZoneId
params.description: The IDs of the Availability Zones

items.docs: Describes the Availability Zones, Local Zones, and Wavelength Zones that are available to you. The results include zones only for the Region you are currently using.
